name: elifetools

metadata.name: elifetools
metadata.summary: Tools for using article data in Python.

documentation:
# elife-tools

Tools for using article data in Python

# Supports

- Python >=3.5

# Non-Python dependencies

- libxml2 (Ubuntu, Arch)

# Install for users

Install via [pip](https://pip.pypa.io/en/stable/):

```bash
$ pip install elifetools
```

You might need to install libxml manually first

```bash
$ sudo STATIC_DEPS=true pip install lxml==3.4.1
```

To install the latest version directly from git

```bash
$ pip install git+https://github.com/elifesciences/elife-tools.git@master
```

or you can add it to your project's requirements.txt file

```bash
git+https://github.com/elifesciences/elife-tools.git@master
```

# Install for developers

Clone the git repo

Make a virtualenv (optional)

Then

```bash
$ python setup.py install
```

# Example usage

```python
>>> from elifetools import parseJATS as parser
>>> soup = parser.parse_document('tests/sample-xml/elife-kitchen-sink.xml')
>>> print(parser.doi(soup))
```

More code examples can be found in `tests/basic_usage_test.py`

# Testing

You can run the full automated test suite from the base folder with:

```bash
$ python -m unittest discover tests
```

or you can run tests with coverage:

```bash
$ coverage run -m unittest discover tests
```

and then view the coverage report:

```bash
$ coverage report -m
```

# License

[The MIT License](http://opensource.org/licenses/mit-license.php)

functions.name: elifetools.utils_html.allowed_xml_tag_fragments
documentation:
tuples of whitelisted tag startswith values for matching tags found in inline text
prior to being converted to HTML
values can be a complete tag for exact matching just the first few characters of a tag
such as the case would be for mml: or table td tags

functions.name: elifetools.utils_html.enhance_xlink_href
documentation: if the href is not prefaced with a protocol, add one

functions.name: elifetools.utils_html.replace_simple_tags
documentation:
Replace tags such as <italic> to <i>
This does not validate markup

functions.name: elifetools.utils_html.list_tag_name
documentation: look at the XML tag list-type attribute to rewrite it to either an HTML ul or ol tag

functions.name: elifetools.utils_html.list_tag_attributes
documentation: rewrite JATS list-type attribute as an HTML class attribute

functions.name: elifetools.utils.peek
documentation: https://stackoverflow.com/questions/661603/how-do-i-know-if-a-generator-is-empty-from-the-start

functions.name: elifetools.utils.coerce_to_int
documentation:
Attempts to cast given value to an integer, return the original value if
failed or the default if one provided.

functions.name: elifetools.utils.date_struct
documentation:
Given year, month and day numeric values and a timezone
convert to structured date object

functions.name: elifetools.utils.date_struct_nn
documentation:
Assemble a date object but if day or month is none set them to 1
to make it easier to deal with partial dates

functions.name: elifetools.utils.day_text
documentation: TODO: if these are being converted to integers perhaps they shouldn't end with '_text' ?

functions.name: elifetools.utils.component_acting_parent_tag
documentation:
Only intended for use in getting components, look for tag name of fig-group
and if so, find the first fig tag inside it as the acting parent tag

functions.name: elifetools.utils.extract_nodes
documentation:
Returns a list of tags (nodes) from the given soup matching the given nodename.
If an optional attribute and value are given, these are used to filter the results
further.

functions.name: elifetools.utils.lazy_extract_nodes
documentation:
Returns a list of tags (nodes) from the given soup matching the given nodename.
If an optional attribute and value are given, these are used to filter the results
further.

functions.name: elifetools.utils.node_contents_str
documentation:
Return the contents of a tag, including it's children, as a string.
Does not include the root/parent of the tag.

functions.name: elifetools.utils.first_parent
documentation:
Given a beautiful soup tag, look at its parents and return the first
tag name that matches nodename or the list nodename

functions.name: elifetools.utils.tag_ordinal
documentation:
Given a beautiful soup tag, look at the tags of the same name that come before it
to get the tag ordinal. For example, if it is tag name fig
and two fig tags are before it, then it is the third fig (3)

functions.name: elifetools.utils.tag_fig_ordinal
documentation:
Meant for finding the position of fig tags with respect to whether
they are for a main figure or a child figure

functions.name: elifetools.utils.tag_sibling_ordinal
documentation:
Given a beautiful soup tag, count the same tags in its siblings
to get a sibling "local" ordinal value. This is useful in counting
child figures within a fig-group, for example

functions.name: elifetools.utils.tag_limit_sibling_ordinal
documentation:
Count previous tags of the same name until it
reaches a tag name of type stop_tag, then stop counting

functions.name: elifetools.utils.tag_media_sibling_ordinal
documentation:
Count sibling ordinal differently depending on if the
mimetype is video or not

functions.name: elifetools.utils.tag_supplementary_material_sibling_ordinal
documentation:
Strategy is to count the previous supplementary-material tags
having the same asset value to get its sibling ordinal.
The result is its position inside any parent tag that
are the same asset type

functions.name: elifetools.utils.supp_asset
documentation:
Given a supplementary-material tag, the asset value depends on
its label text. This also informs in what order (its ordinal) it
has depending on how many of each type is present

functions.name: elifetools.utils.text_to_title
documentation: when a title is required, generate one from the value

functions.name: elifetools.utils.escape_unmatched_angle_brackets
documentation:
In order to make an XML string less malformed, escape
unmatched less than tags that are not part of an allowed tag
Note: Very, very basic, and do not try regex  style replacements
  on unicode ever again! Instead this uses string replace
allowed_tag_fragments is a tuple of tag name matches for use with startswith()

functions.name: elifetools.utils.escape_ampersand
documentation:
Quick convert unicode ampersand characters not associated with
a numbered entity or not starting with allowed characters to a plain &

functions.name: elifetools.utils.list_type_prefix
documentation: from a JATS list list-type attribute return a list style prefix

functions.name: elifetools.xmlio.register_xmlns
documentation: Register namespaces globally

functions.name: elifetools.xmlio.parse
documentation:
to extract the doctype details from the file when parsed and return the data
for later use, set return_doctype_dict to True

functions.name: elifetools.xmlio.add_tag_before
documentation:
Helper function to refactor the adding of new tags
especially for when converting text to role tags

functions.name: elifetools.xmlio.get_first_element_index
documentation:
In order to use Element.insert() in a convenient way,
this function will find the first child tag with tag_name
and return its index position
The index can then be used to insert an element before or after the
found tag using Element.insert()

functions.name: elifetools.xmlio.build_doctype
documentation:
Instantiate an ElifeDocumentType, a subclass of minidom.DocumentType, with
some properties so it is more testable

functions.name: elifetools.xmlio.append_minidom_xml_to_elementtree_xml
documentation:
Recursively,
Given an ElementTree.Element as parent, and a minidom instance as xml,
append the tags and content from xml to parent
Used primarily for adding a snippet of XML with <italic> tags
attributes: a list of attribute names to copy

functions.name: elifetools.xmlio.reparsed_tag
documentation: given tag content and attributes, reparse to a minidom tag

classes.name: elifetools.xmlio.ElifeDocumentType
documentation:
Override minidom.DocumentType in order to get
double quotes in the DOCTYPE rather than single quotes

Due to XML content that will not conform with the strict JSON schema validation rules,
for elife articles only, rewrite the JSON to make it valid

Used to get component DOI from a tag and confirm it is actually for that tag

functions.name: elifetools.parseJATS.components
documentation:
Find the components, i.e. those parts that would be assigned
a unique component DOI, such as figures, tables, etc.
- position is in what order the tag appears in the entire set of nodes
- ordinal is in what order it is for all the tags of its own type

Find the corresp tags included in author-notes

Render the tag as body content and call recursively if

paragraphs may wrap some other body block content

functions.name: elifetools.parseJATS.body_block_title_label_caption
documentation:
set the title, label and caption values in a consistent way

set_caption: insert a "caption" field
prefer_title: when only one value is available,
   set title rather than label. If False, set label rather than title

functions.name: elifetools.parseJATS.lazy_body_blocks
documentation:
Note: for some reason this works and few other attempted methods work
Search for certain node types, find the first nodes siblings of the same type
Add the first sibling and the other siblings to a list and return them

functions.name: elifetools.parseJATS.body_blocks
documentation:
Note: for some reason this works and few other attempted methods work
Search for certain node types, find the first nodes siblings of the same type
Add the first sibling and the other siblings to a list and return them

functions.name: elifetools.parseJATS.authors_json

functions.name: elifetools.parseJATS.author_line
documentation: take preferred names from authors json and format them into an author line

functions.name: elifetools.parseJATS.format_author_line
documentation: authorLine format depends on if there is 1, 2 or more than 2 authors

functions.name: elifetools.parseJATS.format_author_line_etal
documentation:
author line ending in etal if there are many authors
example: Firstname M Lastname et al.

functions.name: elifetools.parseJATS.format_author_line_ellipsis
documentation:
author line with an ellipsis before the final author if there are many authors
example for three authors, max_names=3: First, Second, Third
example for four authors, max_names=3: First, Second ... Fourth

functions.name: elifetools.parseJATS.unwrap_appendix_box
documentation: for use in removing unwanted boxed-content from appendices json
